2026-04-18T18:03:38.0897998Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0898913Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0899636Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0900262Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0901011Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0901410Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0901674Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0901969Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0902252Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0902636Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0902963Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0903685Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-18T18:03:38.0904111Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

The `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs` package added two new properties to `BlobStorageResourceOptions` (`Snapshot` and `VersionId`) for snapshot and version transfer support, but the README has no examples showing how to use them.

2026-04-18T18:06:19.8078047Z **Gap identified:** PR #57368 by @amnguye added `Snapshot` and `VersionId` properties to `BlobStorageResourceOptions` (targeting v12.4.0-beta.1), but the README has no examples showing how to use these properties to transfer from a blob snapshot or version. The implementation guide in the issue covers:

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). responseId\\n- Orchestrator: handler type name on invocation\\n\\nThese logs complement the Core inbound middleware (HTTP-level) with\\nresponse-specific context for debugging storage deserialization issues,\\nhandler failures, and request tracing across the full pipeline.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Update release dates to 2026-04-17\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Address review feedback: exception handling in middleware, cache GetConversationId\\n\\n- InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware: catch exceptions from next() to force\\n  status 500 in log output instead of misleading 200; re-read traceId in\\n  finally block to pick up Activity created later in the pipeline.\\n- ResponseEndpointHandler: cache GetConversationId() result to avoid\\n  repeated JSON parsing across partition key, logging, and session ID.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add x-agent-session-id response header on all protocol endpoints (§8)\\n\\nImplement the spec §8 requirement that every protocol endpoint response\\nMUST include x-agent-session-id as a response header.\\n\\nResponses protocol:\\n- Add SessionIdResponseHeaderFilter endpoint filter (outermost, wraps\\n  exception filter) that reads resolved session ID from HttpContext.Items\\n  and falls back to FoundryEnvironment.SessionId\\n- Store resolved session ID in HttpContext.Items from POST (after B39\\n  resolution), GET, Cancel, and SSE replay handler paths\\n- 12 new E2E protocol tests covering all endpoints, error paths, and\\n  env var fallback\\n\\nInvocations protocol:\\n- Add x-agent-session-id header to GET /invocations/{id} and\\n  POST /invocations/{id}/cancel (POST /invocations already had it)\\n- Add header to GET /invocations/docs/openapi.json with env var fallback\\n- 2 new endpoint tests + header assertions on existing GET/Cancel tests\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Rename ServerUserAgent to ServerVersion and restructure middleware extensions\\n\\nRename all ServerUserAgent* types, files, methods, and properties to\\nServerVersion* — the header identifies the server version stack, not a\\nuser-agent string.\\n\\nType renames:\\n- ServerUserAgentRegistry → ServerVersionRegistry\\n- ServerUserAgentMiddleware → ServerVersionMiddleware\\n- AgentHostBuilder.UserAgentRegistry → VersionRegistry\\n\\nExtension method restructure:\\n- AddAgentServerUserAgent() → AddAgentServerVersion() (version only)\\n- UseAgentServerUserAgent() → UseAgentServerVersion()\\n- NEW: AddAgentServerLogging() — registers InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware\\n- NEW: UseAgentServerLogging() — wires InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware\\n\\nThe old AddAgentServerUserAgent() bundled both version and logging\\nregistration which was confusing. Now each concern has its own pair of\\nAdd/Use methods. Tier 1/2 (AgentHostBuilder) lights up both\\nautomatically; Tier 3 users call them independently.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Update changelogs for session ID header and ServerVersion rename\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add inbound request logging entries to Responses and Invocations changelogs\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Move inbound logging to Features Added in changelogs\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Internalize framework-only telemetry types\\n\\nMake AgentHostTelemetry, InvocationsActivitySource, ResponsesActivitySource,\\nand ResponsesTracingConstants internal. These types are framework plumbing\\nnot needed by handler authors. Reduces public API surface by 128 lines.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Populate session ID in HttpContext.Items for all endpoint paths\\n\\nSSE replay (in-flight), Delete, and InputItems now store the resolved\\nsession ID so SessionIdResponseHeaderFilter uses the actual response's\\nAgentSessionId instead of the FoundryEnvironment fallback.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add session ID header assertions to Delete and InputItems tests\\n\\nBoth tests now set FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID, assert the header is\\npresent, and verify its value — matching the Cancel test pattern.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot ID.\\n\\nFoundryStorageLoggingPolicy success/failure logs now include whether\\nisolation headers were sent on the outbound storage request.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot This prevents leaking account and\\nproject information in diagnostic logs.\\n\\nExample: https://acct.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/myproj/storage/responses/resp_123?api-version=2025-01-01\\u0026token=[REDACTED] ***/storage/responses/resp_123?api-version=2025-01-01\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add server version User-Agent to Foundry storage requests\\n\\nWire ServerVersionPolicy into the storage HTTP pipeline to prepend\\nthe AgentServer identity string (e.g., azure-ai-agentserver-responses/\\n1.0.0-beta.2 (dotnet/10.0)) to the User-Agent header on all outbound\\nFoundry storage API calls.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Use shared ServerVersionRegistry for storage User-Agent\\n\\nResolve the ServerVersionRegistry from DI instead of building a\\nstandalone identity string.
